case_ref: kp_0022 (v1.2 defective item; re-anchored in v1.3)

fable_review.note: Fair reading of a DEFECTIVE item: the v1.2 quote emphasized the recognition PATTERN (amortize/ratable) and hid the deferred-liability nature, so the arm's SAME was reasonable. Fixed in v1.3 (side_a re-anchored to 'sit as an obligation'). Run-2 scores.json is NEVER retro-edited; this exhibit excuses the miss.

model_output.cited_a: Prepaid program dues amortize across the coverage term.
model_output.cited_b: Ratable recognition posts each month of the plan.
model_output.reason: Object: both describe the same underlying thing -- membership/program fee revenue being recognized over time rather than upfront ("prepaid program dues" amortizing = "membership fee income" being recognized). Scope: neither side states company-owned vs franchised, segment vs consolidated, or gross vs net -- this is symmetric silence on both sides, which per the rules is not evidence against sameness. Mechanism: "amortize across the coverage term" and "ratable recognition ... each month of the plan" are two descriptions of the identical causal mechanism -- straight-line/ratable recognition of prepaid membership dues over the membership period -- not a cause vs. a separate downstream effect, just the liability-rollforward view and the income-recognition view of the same accounting process. All three checks are satisfied with no genuine conflict, so this clears the default-DIFFERENT bar.
